scope: Three-peer relay-hop provenance vectors: a forwarded (re-exported) chunk must be authorized at the receiver against its ORIGINAL author, never the relay that forwarded it (review 092 #1). Each case wires whole WorkspaceCores through the public sync_with path and asserts the receiver's apply decision, projection, and audit.
topology: author C -> relay A -> receiver B (B's trust for A and C decides the forwarded chunk's fate)

cases.note: B trusts relay A (Owner) but seeds NO row for author C. C's forwarded write is gated against C, who is unknown to B -> denied, nothing imported, denial names C's source.
The positive twin: B trusts relay A (Owner) AND author C (Editor with db.write on the collection). C's forwarded write is gated against C, who IS trusted -> applied and converges.
B trusts author C as an Editor, but C's db.write does NOT cover the forwarded chunk's collection. The original-author grant check denies even though the relay A is a full Owner.
Control: the relay A authored the chunk ITSELF (no origin_source), so the receiver authorizes the relay A directly. B trusts A (Owner) -> applied.
Control: A authored the chunk locally but B trusts A only as a Viewer. The relay (== author) is gated and the write is denied — provenance handling does not weaken the direct-author path.
